---
title: "06f-gene-expression.Rmd"
output: html_document
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
```{r}
# requiring the required code
require(tidyverse)
if (!require("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE))
    install.packages("BiocManager")

BiocManager::install("DESeq2")
library(DESeq2)
library(ggplot2)
```

```{r}
# importing the dataset for MAC
require(tidyverse)
salmon.data <- read.delim("../../../Downloads/salmon.isoform.counts.matrix", header=TRUE , check.names = FALSE)
```
```{r}
# importing dataset for WINDOWS
require(tidyverse)
salmon.data <- read.delim("../../../../Downloads/salmon.isoform.counts.matrix", header=TRUE , check.names = FALSE)
```

```{r}
colnames(salmon.data)[1] <- "ID"
head(salmon.data)
```
```{r}
#importing the other dataset on MAC
Sequencing.Picks <- read.csv("../metadata/metabolomics-sample-list.csv")
sequencing.picks.8.9.23 <- Sequencing.Picks %>% 
  filter(date == "8/9/23")
```
```{r}
#importing the other dataset on WINDOWS
Sequencing.Picks <- read.csv("../metadata/metabolomics-sample-list.csv")
sequencing.picks.8.9.23 <- Sequencing.Picks %>% 
  filter(date == "8/9/23")
```

```{r}
# making new data subset for end of exp THIS IS WHERE ROW NAMES GETS ADDED(?)
End.data <- salmon.data %>% 
  select(ID, any_of(sequencing.picks.8.9.23$crab.ID)) %>% 
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), round, 0))
head(End.data)
```
```{r}
head(salmon.data)
```

```{r}
nrow(End.data) #Get initial row count: 913680
End.data <- End.data %>%
  mutate(Total = rowSums(.[, 2:35])) %>%
  filter(!Total==0) %>%
  select(!Total) #Remove rows with 0 counts across all samples
nrow(End.data) #Get final row count: 785006
```
```{r}
head(End.data)
```

```{r}
#doing the proper gene analysis
dds.crab <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(countData=End.data, 
                              colData=sequencing.picks.8.9.23, 
                              design=~treatment, tidy = TRUE)
```
```{r}
sf.dds.crab <- estimateSizeFactors(dds.crab) #Estimate size factors to determine if we can use VST to transform our data. Size factors should be less than 4 for us to use vst
print(sizeFactors(sf.dds.crab)) #View size factors
all(sizeFactors(sf.dds.crab)) < 4 #True = we can use VST transformation
```
```{r}
gvst <- vst(sf.dds.crab, blind=FALSE) #Apply a variance stabilizing transformation to minimize effects of small counts and normalize wrt library size
head(assay(gvst), 3) #View transformed gene count data for the first three genes in the dataset.
```

```{r}
#priming the code so once error is fixed, it will take about 5 minutes to fix it
crab.stats <- DESeq(dds.crab)
```
```{r}
results.crab <- results(crab.stats)
head(results(crab.stats, tidy=TRUE))
results.main <- results.crab[order(results.crab$padj),]
head(results.main)
```
```{r}
par(mfrow=c(2,3))

plotCounts(crab.stats, gene="1", intgroup="treatment")
plotCounts(crab.stats, gene="2", intgroup="treatment")
plotCounts(crab.stats, gene="3", intgroup="treatment")
plotCounts(crab.stats, gene="TRINITY_DN7046_c0_g1_i3", intgroup="")
plotCounts(crab.stats, gene="TRINITY_DN12380_c0_g1_i6", intgroup="crab.ID")
plotCounts(crab.stats, gene="TRINITY_DN178823_c0_g1_i1", intgroup="crab.ID")
#... continue from the graph I made upstairs - intgroup is messing me up
```
```{r}
par(mfrow=c(1,1))
with(results.crab, plot(log2FoldChange, -log10(pvalue), pch=20, main="Volcano plot", xlim=c(-3,3)))
with(subset(results.crab, padj<.01 ), points(log2FoldChange, -log10(pvalue), pch=20, col="blue"))
with(subset(results.crab, padj<.01 & abs(log2FoldChange)>2), points(log2FoldChange, -log10(pvalue), pch=20, col="red")) #negative log2fold = underexpressed/downregulated, visa versa
```
```{r}
gvst <- vst(crab.stats, blind=FALSE)
plotPCA(gvst, intgroup="treatment") # horizontal is temp-driven changes in gene expression
```

### `arrayQualityMetrics`

```{r}
#e <- ExpressionSet(assay(gvst), AnnotatedDataFrame(as.data.frame(colData(gvst)))) #Generate expression set for outlier identification
#arrayQualityMetrics(e, intgroup = c("condition"), force = T, outdir = "report_for_genes_Loaded") #Identify outliers.

#Based on the report, Apalm_13, (adjacent), Apalm_17 (adjacent), and Apalm_27 (hypoxic) are outliers based on distances between arrays.
```

### Remove outliers

Apalm_27 was flagged as overrepresented sequences after trimming by FastQC and has a higher percentage of multiple mappings than any other sample. This gives me good reason to remove Apalm_27. Apalm_13 and Apalm_17 have no other reasons to be outliers, so I will keep them in the analysis.

```{r}
gcount <- gcount %>% select(-c(Apalm_27)) #Remove outliers
metadata <- metadata %>% dplyr::slice(-c(12)) #Remove outliers
```
